name: Probability and Statistics
tagline: Reasoning under uncertainty for measurement, decisions, and learning from data
problem:
Real measurements are noisy and incomplete. Without a framework for uncertainty, you cannot distinguish signal from coincidence or design robust systems that survive variation.

Probability models uncertainty; statistics turns observations into estimates with error bars. This is central to medicine, engineering safety, and machine learning.

The remaining problem for Probability and Statistics is operational reliability. A reader needs more than a name and a historical note: they need to know what failure this capability prevents, what resources it consumes, what signs show that it is failing, and when it is safer to stop than to improvise. This entry therefore treats probability and statistics as a teachable system with inputs, inspection points, records, and limits rather than as a single trick.
overview:
Probability assigns models to random variables and events; statistics uses data to fit models, test hypotheses, and estimate parameters. The discipline is as much about avoiding self-deception as it is about formulas.

For AI, statistics governs generalization, evaluation, and calibration. For physical systems, it governs tolerances, reliability, and risk management.

In collapse recovery, probability and statistics must be repairable with local materials, teachable to more than one person, and documented well enough that the method survives injury, migration, or leadership change. In a space-settlement context, the same capability must account for closed-loop resources, contamination control, spare-part scarcity, power budgets, and the cost of mistakes in a sealed habitat. The practical minimum is a controlled trial, written checks, safe storage, and review before scale-up.

principles.name: Random variables
principles.explanation: Model uncertain quantities; distributions encode plausible ranges and likelihoods.

principles.name: Estimation
principles.explanation: Infer parameters from data with bias/variance tradeoffs and confidence intervals.

principles.name: Hypothesis testing
principles.explanation: Evaluate evidence; interpret p-values and effect sizes carefully to avoid false certainty.

components.name: Use-case boundary
components.function: Defines what Probability and Statistics is meant to accomplish and what it must not be used for
components.madeFrom: Written requirements, hazard notes, local constraints, and responsible operator assignment
components.criticalNote: A vague use case leads to unsafe improvisation.

components.name: Input control point
components.function: Checks materials, tools, information, and environmental conditions before work starts
components.position: At receiving, storage, or setup area
components.madeFrom: Labels, inspection notes, gauges, samples, and quarantine bins
components.criticalNote: Unknown inputs should be isolated until checked.

rawMaterials.name: Paper and writing media
rawMaterials.purpose: Record calculations, assumptions, and experimental outcomes
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Digital notes plus printed backups; radiation-safe storage for critical references.

rawMaterials.name: Measurement tools
rawMaterials.purpose: Quantify reality so models are not guesswork
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Prefer multi-use instruments; maintain calibration artifacts and procedures.

rawMaterials.name: Time and attention
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Training time is a hard constraint; plan curricula and drills early.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Deliberate practice, peer review, and documentation.

rawMaterials.name: Electric power
rawMaterials.purpose: Energy for computation, storage, and networking
earthLocations: Electrical grids globally; generators in remote sites
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Solar + batteries is the simplest early-space pathway; plan for tight energy budgets and thermal rejection.
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Stable voltage regulation, grounding, and basic power-quality control.

buildSteps.title: Define uncertainty explicitly
buildSteps.description: Write what is random, what is fixed, and what you assume about noise.

buildSteps.title: Collect representative samples
buildSteps.description: Design sampling to match the question; record context and confounders.
buildSteps.warningNote: Biased sampling can overwhelm any analysis.

buildSteps.title: Report error bounds
buildSteps.description: Use confidence intervals or credible intervals; include effect sizes, not just significance.
buildSteps.warningNote: Point estimates without uncertainty invite disaster.

buildSteps.title: Define the local purpose and stop conditions
buildSteps.description: Write down why Probability and Statistics is needed, who is responsible, what success looks like, and which conditions require stopping or escalation.
buildSteps.warningNote: Do not start a life-critical or infrastructure-critical use without clear stop conditions.

buildSteps.title: Inspect inputs before use
buildSteps.description: Check materials, tools, water, power, workspace, records, and operator readiness. Mark substitutions and unknowns before work begins.
buildSteps.warningNote: Substitutions can introduce contamination, fire, structural, electrical, medical, or chemical hazards.

history.event: Probability formalizes; statistics emerges with astronomy and social measurement.
Modern statistical inference and experimental design mature.
Data-driven methods become central across domains.

impact:
Probability and statistics make uncertainty manageable and decisions auditable. They reduce superstition and improve safety when used honestly.

In The Codex, they are prerequisites for reliable medicine, engineering, and machine learning systems.

sources.title: Jaynes — Probability Theory: The Logic of Science
